---
title: "Data"
subtitle: "How We Prepared Our Dataset"
---

## 1. Where We Got Our Data

To analyze the trends of Industrial Engineering programs in Turkey, our team combined two different data sources. We wanted to see the changes over a long period (2018-2024).

-   **YÖK Atlas Data (2018-2020):** We used the `thestats` R package created by M. Çavuş and O. Aydın. It helped us pull the official placement data directly into R.
-   **Kaggle Dataset (2021-2024):** To get more recent data and extra details like total preference counts and city information, we downloaded a dataset from Kaggle.

## 2. Data Cleaning and Wrangling

Raw admission data is usually messy. University names change or contain typos, and there are many unrelated departments with "Engineering" in their name. We used `tidyverse` for data manipulation:

-   **Filtering:** We only kept Industrial Engineering departments. We had to specifically remove "Woodworking" or "Forestry" engineering programs that popped up in our search.
-   **Fixing Names:** We changed Turkish characters and standard words (like converting "Üniversitesi" to "UNIVERSITY") so we could join the two datasets properly.
-   **Creating New Variables:** We calculated a `Demand_Ratio` by dividing Total Preferences by Quota. This helps us see how popular a program really is.
-   **Grouping:** We separated universities into "State" and "Foundation" to compare them later in our EDA.

## 3. Data Dictionary

Here are the most important columns in our final merged dataset:

| Variable | Meaning |
|:-----------------------------------|:-----------------------------------|
| **Year** | Academic year (from 2018 to 2024) |
| **University_Name** | Standardized name of the university |
| **Rank** | National base placement rank (lower number is better) |
| **Demand_Ratio** | Preferences / Quota (How many students applied per seat) |
| **Top1_Pref** | How many students put this department as their first choice |

## 4. Final Dataset

As requested in the project guidelines, we saved our final merged data as an `.RData` file. You can download it below to reproduce our analysis.

[**Download Final Processed Data (.RData)**](data/ie_combined_final.RData)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. Our R Code

Below is the code we wrote to pull the data, clean it, and merge it together.

\`\`\`{r} #\| label: data-cleaning #\| message: false #\| warning: false #\| code-fold: true

# We need these libraries first

library(tidyverse) library(thestats)

# --- Step 1: Get YÖK Atlas Data (2018-2020) ---

raw_thestats \<- list_score(department_names = "Industrial Engineering", lang = "en")

clean_thestats \<- raw_thestats %\>% filter(as.numeric(year) \<= 2020) %\>% \# Removing unrelated engineering programs that have similar names filter(!str_detect(str_to_lower(department), "woodworking\|fisheries\|aquaculture\|forest\|design")) %\>% mutate(University_Type = case_when( str_detect(str_to_lower(type), "devlet\|state") \~ "State", str_detect(str_to_lower(type), "vak\|foundation\|private") \~ "Foundation", TRUE \~ "Other")) %\>% filter(University_Type != "Other") %\>% select(Year = year, University_Type, University_Name = university, Faculty_Name = faculty, Department_Name = department) %\>% mutate(Year = as.numeric(Year))

# --- Step 2: Get Kaggle Data (2021-2024) ---

raw_kaggle \<- read_csv("data/01_university_admissions_turkey_2019_2024.csv")

clean_kaggle \<- raw_kaggle %\>% filter(as.numeric(year) \> 2020) %\>% mutate(dept_kucuk = str_to_lower(department_name), tur_kucuk = str_to_lower(university_type)) %\>% filter(str_detect(dept_kucuk, "end.str") & str_detect(dept_kucuk, "m.hendis"), !str_detect(dept_kucuk, "orman\|a.a.\|tasar.m\|su .r.nleri")) %\>% \# Standardizing names so we can join data frames later mutate(University_Type = case_when( str_detect(tur_kucuk, "devlet\|state\|kamu") \~ "State", str_detect(tur_kucuk, "vak\|foundation\|.zel\|private") \~ "Foundation", TRUE \~ "Other"), university_name = str_replace_all(university_name, "(?i).n.vers.tes.", "UNIVERSITY"), faculty_name = str_replace_all(faculty_name, "(?i)m.hend.sl.k", "Engineering"), department_name = str_replace_all(department_name, "(?i)end.str.", "Industrial")) %\>% filter(University_Type != "Other") %\>% select(Year = year, City = city, University_Type, University_Name = university_name, Faculty_Name = faculty_name, Department_Name = department_name, Rank = final_rank_012, Quota = total_quota, Preferences = total_preferences, Demand_Ratio = demand_per_quota, Top1_Pref = top_1_pref_count) %\>% mutate(across(c(Year, Rank, Quota, Preferences, Demand_Ratio, Top1_Pref), as.numeric))

# --- Step 3: Combine and Save ---

ie_combined \<- bind_rows(clean_thestats, clean_kaggle) %\>% mutate(across(c(University_Name, Faculty_Name, Department_Name), \~str_to_title(str_squish(.)))) %\>% arrange(desc(Year), University_Name)

# Save to data folder so we can use it in our analysis page

save(ie_combined, file = "data/ie_combined_final.RData")
